expectations.text: Respects the no-scan constraint and does not claim concrete obmm API facts beyond the name obmm.
expectations.evidence: States it did not explore/read obmm sources and marks facts Unknown.

expectations.text: Produces organized design documentation with clear sections or files for architecture, binding/build, safe API, testing/release, and async/protocol analysis when applicable.
expectations.evidence: Organized into architecture, binding, safe API, async and checklist sections.

expectations.text: Uses Unknown or unresolved items for C API shape, resource model, errors, threading, build metadata, and license.
expectations.evidence: Uses Unknown for API shape, resources, errors, threading, build model and license.

expectations.text: Provides a conservative obmm-sys plus obmm default while explaining what evidence could change the decision.
expectations.evidence: Defaults to obmm-sys plus obmm and explains evidence that could change decisions.

expectations.text: Makes binding strategy explicit and names excluded alternatives.
expectations.evidence: Explicitly chooses pre-generated bindgen as a provisional path and excludes build-time bindgen, pure handwritten and wrapper.c defaults.

expectations.text: Covers build.rs/linking, docs.rs/CI fallback, safe wrappers, Error/Result, unsafe boundaries, Send/Sync, tests, and release checks.
expectations.evidence: Covers linking, docs.rs/CI fallback, safe wrappers, Error/Result, unsafe boundaries, Send/Sync, tests and release.

expectations.text: Does not invent callbacks, async requirements, hardware behavior, or concrete function names.
expectations.evidence: Does not claim concrete obmm function names; examples are clearly placeholder patterns/prefixes.

expectations.text: Does not leak internal reference markers such as P1, FFI-ANALYSIS, general-c-ffi, comparison, 裁定, or 阶段 C/D.
expectations.evidence: No forbidden internal reference marker appears in the design output.

uncertainties: Timing/token metrics were not available from subagent notifications in this environment.
